element_introduction_order.mastery_context: demonstrated against Tyrannosaurus boss in first level

typical_relief_triggers: defeating boss and choosing next stage route
reaching checkpoint areas with weapon pickups
typical_tension_triggers: boss battles with health drain mechanic
continuous enemy spawns during run-and-gun sequences
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: alternating run-and-gun action sequences with platforming challenges; health drain system creates constant low-level tension that escalates during boss encounters

difficulty_curve.early_game: gentle introduction with basic platforming and first weapon (boomerang) plus stone wheel demonstration against Tyrannosaurus
difficulty_curve.mid_game: weapon variety expands (fire, flints, electricity, clubs); enemy complexity increases; health drain system pressure intensifies
difficulty_curve.late_game: accumulated weapon mastery required; boss difficulty escalates; final boss presents multiple ending paths based on performance
notable_spikes: first boss encounter (Tyrannosaurus) establishes combat expectations
final boss with multiple ending routes creates strategic replay tension

notable_level_patterns.description: After defeating boss encounters, players select between different routes, creating emergent level variety and replayability. Genesis version (close arcade match) preserves this feature.
Levels feature significant platforming sections (particularly in SNES version which extended them further) interspersed with run-and-gun combat sequences
notable_level_patterns.example_level: prehistoric themed worlds with environmental hazards

notable_level_patterns.description: Continuous health loss outside boss battles creates momentum pressure; players must maintain forward progress rather than backtracking or camping
